column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en
BG 7.B#ॐ श्री परमात्मने नमः\nअथ सप्तमः अध्यायः ज्ञान विज्ञान योगः#om sri paramatmane namaha\natha saptama adhyayaha jnana vijnana yogaha#Salutations to the most auspicious divine in all. Henceforth
column2: the seventh chapter named Jnana Vijnana Yoga#om sri paramatmane—the most auspicious divine in all; namaha—salutations; atha—henceforth; saptamodhyayaha—seventh chapter; jnana vijnana yogaha—Yoga of knowledge and realisation#

column1:
BG 7.1#श्रीभगवान् उवाच |
मयि आसक्त मनाः पार्थ 
योगं युञ्जन् मद् आश्रयः |
असंशयं समग्रं माम् 
यथा ज्ञास्यसि तत् शृणु ||1||#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
mayyāsakta-manāḥ pārtha yogaṁ yuñjan mad-āśhrayaḥ
asanśhayaṁ samagraṁ māṁ yathā jñāsyasi tach chhṛiṇu#The Supreme Lord said: Now listen
column2: O Arjuna
column3: how
column4: with the mind attached exclusively to Me
column5: and surrendering to Me through the practice of bhakti yog
column7: free from doubt.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Lord said; mayi—to me; āsakta-manāḥ—with the mind attached; pārtha—Arjuna
column8: the son of Pritha; yogam—bhakti yog; yuñjan—practicing; mad-āśhrayaḥ—surrendering to me; asanśhayam—free from doubt; samagram—completely; mām—me; yathā—how; jñāsyasi—you shall know; tat—that; śhṛiṇu—listen#

column1:
BG 7.2#ज्ञानं ते अहं स विज्ञानम् 
इदं वक्ष्यामि अशेषतः |
यत् ज्ञात्वा न इह भूयः 
अन्यत् ज्ञातव्यम् अवशिष्यते ||2||#jñānaṁ te ’haṁ sa-vijñānam idaṁ vakṣhyāmyaśheṣhataḥ
yaj jñātvā neha bhūyo ’nyaj jñātavyam-avaśhiṣhyate#I shall now reveal unto you fully this knowledge and wisdom
column2: knowing which nothing else remains to be known in this world.#jñānam—knowledge; te—unto you; aham—I; sa—with; vijñānam—wisdom; idam—this; vakṣhyāmi—shall reveal; aśheṣhataḥ—in full; yat—which; jñātvā—having known; na—not; iha—in this world; bhūyaḥ—further; anyat—anything else; jñātavyam—to be known; avaśhiṣhyate—remains#

column1:
BG 7.3#मनुष्याणाम् सहस्रेषु 
कश्चित् यतति सिद्धये |
यतताम् अपि सिद्धानाम् 
कश्चित् माम् वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ||3||#manuṣhyāṇāṁ sahasreṣhu kaśhchid yatati siddhaye
yatatām api siddhānāṁ kaśhchin māṁ vetti tattvataḥ#Amongst thousands of persons
column2: hardly one strives for perfection; and amongst those who have achieved perfection
column3: hardly one knows Me in truth.#manuṣhyāṇām—of men; sahasreṣhu—out of many thousands; kaśhchit—someone; yatati—strives; siddhaye—for perfection; yatatām—of those who strive; api—even; siddhānām—of those who have achieved perfection; kaśhchit—someone; mām—me; vetti—knows; tattvataḥ—in truth#

column1:
BG 7.4#भूमिः आपः अनलः वायुः 
खम् मनः बुद्धिः एव च |
अहङ्कारः इति इयं मे 
भिन्ना प्रकृतिः अष्टधा ||4||#bhūmir-āpo ’nalo vāyuḥ khaṁ mano buddhir eva cha
ahankāra itīyaṁ me bhinnā prakṛitir aṣhṭadhā#Earth
column2: water
column3: fire
column8: and ego—these are eight components of My material energy.#bhūmiḥ—earth; āpaḥ—water; analaḥ—fire; vāyuḥ—air; kham—space; manaḥ—mind; buddhiḥ—intellect; eva—certainly; cha—and; ahankāraḥ—ego; iti—thus; iyam—all these; me—my; bhinnā—divisions; prakṛitiḥ—material energy; aṣhṭadhā—eightfold#

column1:
BG 7.5#अपरा इयम् इतः तु अन्याम् 
प्रकृतिम् विद्धि मे पराम् |
जीव भूताम् महाबाहो 
यया इदं धार्यते जगत् ||5||#apareyam itas tvanyāṁ prakṛitiṁ viddhi me parām
jīva-bhūtāṁ mahā-bāho yayedaṁ dhāryate jagat#Such is My inferior energy. But beyond it
column2: O mighty-armed Arjuna
column3: I have a superior energy. This is the jīva śhakti (the soul energy)
column4: which comprises the embodied souls who are the basis of life in this world.#aparā—inferior; iyam—this; itaḥ—besides this; tu—but; anyām—another; prakṛitim—energy; viddhi—know; me—my; parām—superior; jīva-bhūtām—living beings; mahā-bāho—mighty-armed one; yayā—by whom; idam—this; dhāryate—the basis; jagat—the material world#

column1:
BG 7.6#एतत् योनीनि भूतानि 
सर्वाणि इति उपधारय |
अहम् कृत्स्नस्य जगतः 
प्रभवः प्रलयः तथा ||6||#etad-yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇītyupadhāraya
ahaṁ kṛitsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā#Know that all living beings are manifested by these two energies of Mine. I am the source of the entire creation
column2: and into Me it again dissolves.#etat yonīni—these two (energies) are the source of; bhūtāni—living beings; sarvāṇi—all; iti—that; upadhāraya—know; aham—I; kṛitsnasya—entire; jagataḥ—creation; prabhavaḥ—the source; pralayaḥ—dissolution; tathā—and#

column1:
BG 7.7#मत्त: परतरम् न अन्यत् 
किञ्चित् अस्ति धनञ्जय |
मयि सर्वम् इदं प्रोतम् 
सूत्रे मणि गणाः इव ||7||#mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat kiñchid asti dhanañjaya
mayi sarvam idaṁ protaṁ sūtre maṇi-gaṇā iva#There is nothing higher than Myself
column2: O Arjun. Everything rests in Me
column3: as beads strung on a thread.#mattaḥ—than me; para-taram—superior; na—not; anyat kiñchit—anything else; asti—there is; dhanañjaya—Arjuna
column4: conqueror of wealth; mayi—in me; sarvam—all; idam—which we see; protam—is strung; sūtre—on a thread; maṇi-gaṇāḥ—beads; iva—like#

column1:
BG 7.8#रसः अहम् अप्सु कौन्तेय 
प्रभा अस्मि शशि सूर्ययोः |
प्रणवः सर्व वेदेषु 
शब्दः खे पौरुषम् नृषु ||8||#raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śhaśhi-sūryayoḥ
praṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣhu śhabdaḥ khe pauruṣhaṁ nṛiṣhu#I am the taste in water
column2: O son of Kunti
column3: and the radiance of the sun and the moon. I am the sacred syllable Om in the Vedic mantras; I am the sound in ether
column4: and the ability in humans.#rasaḥ—taste; aham—I; apsu—in water; kaunteya—Arjuna
column5: the son of Kunti; prabhā—the radiance; asmi—I am; śhaśhi-sūryayoḥ—of the moon and the sun; praṇavaḥ—the sacred syllable Om; sarva—in all; vedeṣhu—Vedas; śhabdaḥ—sound; khe—in ether; pauruṣham—ability; nṛiṣhu—in humans#

column1:
BG 7.9#पुण्यः गन्धः पृथिव्याम् च 
तेजः च अस्मि विभावसौ |
जीवनम् सर्व भूतेषु 
तपः च अस्मि तपस्विषु ||9||#puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛithivyāṁ cha tejaśh chāsmi vibhāvasau
jīvanaṁ sarva-bhūteṣhu tapaśh chāsmi tapasviṣhu#I am the pure fragrance of the Earth
column2: and the brilliance in fire. I am the life-force in all beings
column3: and the penance of the ascetics.#puṇyaḥ—pure; gandhaḥ—fragrance; pṛithivyām—of the earth; cha—and; tejaḥ—brilliance; cha—and; asmi—I am; vibhāvasau—in the fire; jīvanam—the life-force; sarva—in all; bhūteṣhu—beings; tapaḥ—penance; cha—and; asmi—I am; tapasviṣhu—of the ascetics#

column1:
BG 7.10#बीजम् माम् सर्व भूतानाम् 
विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् |
बुद्धिः बुद्धिमताम् अस्मि 
तेजः तेजस्विनाम् अहम् ||10||#bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanam
buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham#O Arjuna
column2: know that I am the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intellect of the intelligent
column3: and the splendor of the glorious.#bījam—the seed; mām—me; sarva-bhūtānām—of all beings; viddhi—know; pārtha—Arjuna
column4: the son of Pritha; sanātanam—the eternal; buddhiḥ—intellect; buddhi-matām—of the intelligent; asmi—(I) am; tejaḥ—splendor; tejasvinām—of the splendid; aham—I#

column1:
BG 7.11#बलम् बलवताम् च अहम् 
काम राग विवर्जितम् |
धर्म अविरुद्धः भूतेषु 
कामः अस्मि भरतर्षभ ||11||#balaṁ balavatāṁ chāhaṁ kāma-rāga-vivarjitam
dharmāviruddho bhūteṣhu kāmo ’smi bharatarṣhabha#O best of the Bharatas
column2: in strong persons
column3: I am their strength devoid of desire and passion. I am sexual activity not conflicting with virtue or scriptural injunctions.#balam—strength; bala-vatām—of the strong; cha—and; aham—I; kāma—desire; rāga—passion; vivarjitam—devoid of; dharma-aviruddhaḥ—not conflicting with dharma; bhūteṣhu—in all beings; kāmaḥ—sexual activity; asmi—(I) am; bharata-ṛiṣhabha—Arjuna

column1:
BG 7.12#ये च एव सात्त्विकाः भावाः 
राजसाः तामसाः च ये |
मत्त एव इति तान् विद्धि 
न तु अहम् तेषु ते मयि ||12||#ye chaiva sāttvikā bhāvā rājasās tāmasāśh cha ye
matta eveti tān viddhi na tvahaṁ teṣhu te mayi#The three states of material existence—goodness
column2: passion
column3: and ignorance—are manifested by My energy. They are in Me
column4: but I am beyond them.#ye—whatever; cha—and; eva—certainly; sāttvikāḥ—in the mode of goodness; bhāvāḥ—states of material existence; rājasāḥ—in the mode of passion; tāmasāḥ—in the mode of ignorance; cha—and; ye—whatever; mattaḥ—from me; eva—certainly; iti—thus; tān—those; viddhi—know; na—not; tu—but; aham—I; teṣhu—in them; te—they; mayi—in me#

column1:
BG 7.13#त्रिभिः गुणमयैः भावैः 
एभिः सर्वम् इदं जगत् |
मोहितम् न अभिजानाति 
माम् एभ्यः परम् अव्ययम् ||13||#tribhir guṇa-mayair bhāvair ebhiḥ sarvam idaṁ jagat
mohitaṁ nābhijānāti māmebhyaḥ param avyayam#Deluded by the three modes of Maya
column2: people in this world are unable to know Me
column3: the imperishable and eternal.#tribhiḥ—by three; guṇa-mayaiḥ—consisting of the modes of material nature; bhāvaiḥ—states; ebhiḥ—all these; sarvam—whole; idam—this; jagat—universe; mohitam—deluded; na—not; abhijānāti—know; mām—me; ebhyaḥ—these; param—the supreme; avyayam—imperishable#

column1:
BG 7.14#दैवी हि एषा गुणमयी 
मम माया दुरत्यया |
माम् एव ये प्रपद्यन्ते 
मायाम् एताम् तरन्ति ते ||14||#daivī hyeṣhā guṇa-mayī mama māyā duratyayā
mām eva ye prapadyante māyām etāṁ taranti te#My divine energy Maya
column2: consisting of the three modes of nature
column3: is very difficult to overcome. But those who surrender unto Me cross over it easily.#daivī—divine; hi—certainly; eṣhā—this; guṇa-mayī—consisting of the three modes of nature; mama—my; māyā—God’s external energy; duratyayā—very difficult to overcome; mām—unto me; eva—certainly; ye—who; prapadyante—surrender; māyām etām—this Maya; taranti—cross over; te—they#

column1:
BG 7.15#न माम् दुष्कृतिनः मूढाः 
प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः |
मायया अपहृत ज्ञानाः 
आसुरम् भावम् आश्रिताः ||15||#na māṁ duṣhkṛitino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥ
māyayāpahṛita-jñānā āsuraṁ bhāvam āśhritāḥ#Four kinds of people do not surrender unto Me—those ignorant of knowledge
column2: those who lazily follow their lower nature though capable of knowing Me
column3: those with deluded intellect
column4: and those with a demoniac nature.#na—not; mām—unto me; duṣhkṛitinaḥ—the evil doers; mūḍhāḥ—the ignorant; prapadyante—surrender; nara-adhamāḥ—one who lazily follows one’s lower nature; māyayā—by God’s material energy; apahṛita jñānāḥ—those with deluded intellect; āsuram—demoniac; bhāvam—nature; āśhritāḥ—surrender#

column1:
BG 7.16#चतुर्विधाः भजन्ते माम् 
जनाः सुकृतिनः अर्जुन |
आर्तः जिज्ञासुः अर्थार्थी 
ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ ||16||#chatur-vidhā bhajante māṁ janāḥ sukṛitino ’rjuna
ārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī cha bharatarṣhabha#O best amongst the Bharatas
column2: four kinds of pious people engage in My devotion—the distressed
column3: the seekers of knowledge
column5: and those who are situated in knowledge.#chatuḥ-vidhāḥ—four kinds; bhajante—worship; mām—me; janāḥ—people; su-kṛitinaḥ—those who are pious; arjuna—Arjun; ārtaḥ—the distressed; jijñāsuḥ—the seekers of knowledge; artha-arthī—the seekers of material gain; jñānī—those who are situated in knowledge; cha—and; bharata-ṛiṣhabha—The best amongst the Bharatas

column1:
BG 7.17#तेषाम् ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्तः 
एक भक्तिः विशिष्यते |
प्रियः हि ज्ञानिनः अत्यर्थम् 
अहम् सः च मम प्रियः ||17||#teṣhāṁ jñānī nitya-yukta eka-bhaktir viśhiṣhyate
priyo hi jñānino ’tyartham ahaṁ sa cha mama priyaḥ#Amongst these
column2: I consider them to be the highest
column3: who worship Me with knowledge
column4: and are steadfastly and exclusively devoted to Me. I am very dear to them and they are very dear to Me.#teṣhām—amongst these; jñānī—those who are situated in knowledge; nitya-yuktaḥ—ever steadfast; eka—exclusively; bhaktiḥ—devotion; viśhiṣhyate—highest; priyaḥ—very dear; hi—certainly; jñāninaḥ—to the person in knowledge; atyartham—highly; aham—I; saḥ—he; cha—and; mama—to me; priyaḥ—very dear#

column1:
BG 7.18#उदाराः सर्व एव एते 
ज्ञानी तु आत्मैव मे मतम् |
आस्थितः सः हि युक्तात्मा 
माम् एव अनुत्तमाम् गतिम् ||18||#udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tvātmaiva me matam
āsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā mām evānuttamāṁ gatim#All those who are devoted to Me are indeed noble. But those in knowledge
column2: who are of steadfast mind
column3: whose intellect is merged in Me
column4: and who have made Me alone as their supreme goal
column5: I consider as My very self.#udārāḥ—noble; sarve—all; eva—indeed; ete—these; jñānī—those in knowledge; tu—but; ātmā eva—my very self; me—my; matam—opinion; āsthitaḥ—situated; saḥ—he; hi—certainly; yukta-ātmā—those who are united; mām—in me; eva—certainly; anuttamām—the supreme; gatim—goal#

column1:
BG 7.19#बहूनाम् जन्मनाम् अन्ते 
ज्ञानवान् माम् प्रपद्यते |
वासुदेवः सर्वम् इति 
सः महात्मा सुदुर्लभः ||19||#bahūnāṁ janmanām ante jñānavān māṁ prapadyate
vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā su-durlabhaḥ#After many births of spiritual practice
column2: one who is endowed with knowledge surrenders unto Me
column3: knowing Me to be all that is. Such a great soul is indeed very rare.#bahūnām—many; janmanām—births; ante—after; jñāna-vān—one who is endowed with knowledge; mām—unto me; prapadyate—surrenders; vāsudevaḥ—Shree Krishna
column4: the son of Vasudev; sarvam—all; iti—that; saḥ—that; mahā-ātmā—great soul; su-durlabhaḥ—very rare#

column1:
BG 7.20#कामैः तैः तैः हृत ज्ञानाः 
प्रपद्यन्ते अन्य देवताः |
तम् तम् नियमम् आस्थाय 
प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया ||20||#kāmais tais tair hṛita-jñānāḥ prapadyante ’nya-devatāḥ
taṁ taṁ niyamam āsthāya prakṛityā niyatāḥ svayā#Those whose knowledge has been carried away by material desires surrender to the celestial gods. Following their own nature
column2: they worship the devatās
column3: practicing rituals meant to propitiate these celestial personalities.#kāmaiḥ—by material desires; taiḥ taiḥ—by various; hṛita-jñānāḥ—whose knowledge has been carried away; prapadyante—surrender; anya—to other; devatāḥ—celestial gods; tam tam—the various; niyamam—rules and regulations; āsthāya—following; prakṛityā—by nature; niyatāḥ—controlled; svayā—by their own#

column1:
BG 7.21#यः यः याम् याम् तनुम् भक्तः 
श्रद्धया अर्चितुम् इच्छति |
तस्य तस्य अचलाम् श्रद्धाम् 
ताम् एव विदधामि अहम् ||21||#yo yo yāṁ yāṁ tanuṁ bhaktaḥ śhraddhayārchitum ichchhati
tasya tasyāchalāṁ śhraddhāṁ tām eva vidadhāmyaham#Whatever celestial form a devotee seeks to worship with faith
column2: I steady the faith of such a devotee in that form.#yaḥ yaḥ—whoever; yām yām—whichever; tanum—form; bhaktaḥ—devotee; śhraddhayā—with faith; architum—to worship; ichchhati—desires; tasya tasya—to him; achalām—steady; śhraddhām—faith; tām—in that; eva—certainly; vidadhāmi—bestow; aham—I#

column1:
BG 7.22#सः तया श्रद्धया युक्तः 
तस्य आराधनम् ईहते |
लभते च ततः कामान् 
मया एव विहितान् हि तान् ||22||#sa tayā śhraddhayā yuktas tasyārādhanam īhate
labhate cha tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān#Endowed with faith
column2: the devotee worships a particular celestial god and obtains the objects of desire. But in reality
column3: I alone arrange these benefits.#saḥ—he; tayā—with that; śhraddhayā—faith; yuktaḥ—endowed with; tasya—of that; ārādhanam—worship; īhate—tries to engage in; labhate—obtains; cha—and; tataḥ—from that; kāmān—desires; mayā—by me; eva—alone; vihitān—granted; hi—certainly; tān—those#

column1:
BG 7.23#अन्तवत् तु फलम् तेषाम् 
तत् भवति अल्प मेधसाम् |
देवान् देव यजः यान्ति 
मद् भक्ताः यान्ति माम् अपि ||23||#antavat tu phalaṁ teṣhāṁ tad bhavatyalpa-medhasām
devān deva-yajo yānti mad-bhaktā yānti mām api#But the fruit gained by these people of little understanding is perishable. Those who worship the celestial gods go to the celestial abodes
column2: while My devotees come to Me.#anta-vat—perishable; tu—but; phalam—fruit; teṣhām—by them; tat—that; bhavati—is; alpa-medhasām—people of little understanding; devān—to the celestial gods; deva-yajaḥ—the worshipers of the celestial gods; yānti—go; mat—my; bhaktāḥ—devotees; yānti—go; mām—to me; api—whereas#

column1:
BG 7.24#अव्यक्तम् व्यक्तिम् आपन्नम् 
मन्यन्ते माम् अबुद्धयः |
परम् भावम् अजानन्तः 
मम अव्ययम् अनुत्तमम् ||24||#avyaktaṁ vyaktim āpannaṁ manyante mām abuddhayaḥ
paraṁ bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam#The less intelligent think that I
column2: the Supreme Lord Shree Krishna
column3: was formless earlier and have now assumed this personality. They do not understand the imperishable exalted nature of My personal form.#avyaktam—formless; vyaktim—possessing a personality; āpannam—to have assumed; manyante—think; mām—me; abuddhayaḥ—less intelligent; param—Supreme; bhāvam—nature; ajānantaḥ—not understanding; mama—my; avyayam—imperishable; anuttamam—excellent#

column1:
BG 7.25#न अहम् प्रकाशः सर्वस्य 
योग माया समावृतः |
मूढः अयम् न अभिजानाति 
लोकः माम् अजम् अव्ययम् ||25||#nāhaṁ prakāśhaḥ sarvasya yoga-māyā-samāvṛitaḥ
mūḍho ’yaṁ nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam#I am not manifest to everyone
column2: being veiled by My divine Yogmaya energy. Hence
column3: those without knowledge do not know that I am without birth and changeless.#na—not; aham—I; prakāśhaḥ—manifest; sarvasya—to everyone; yoga-māyā—God’s supreme (divine) energy; samāvṛitaḥ—veiled; mūḍhaḥ—deluded; ayam—these; na—not; abhijānāti—know; lokaḥ—persons; mām—me; ajam—unborn; avyayam—immutable#

column1:
BG 7.26#वेद अहम् समतीतानि 
वर्तमानानि च अर्जुन |
भविष्याणि च भूतानि 
माम् तु वेद न कश्चन ||26||#vedāhaṁ samatītāni vartamānāni chārjuna
bhaviṣhyāṇi cha bhūtāni māṁ tu veda na kaśhchana#O Arjuna
column2: I know of past
column3: present
column5: and I also know all living beings; but Me no one knows.#veda—know; aham—I; samatītāni—the past; vartamānāni—the present; cha—and; arjuna—Arjun; bhaviṣhyāṇi—the future; cha—also; bhūtāni—all living beings; mām—me; tu—but; veda—knows; na kaśhchana—no one#

column1:
BG 7.27#इच्छा द्वेष समुत्थेन 
द्वन्द्व मोहेन भारत |
सर्व भूतानि सम्मोहम् 
सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप ||27||#ichchhā-dveṣha-samutthena dvandva-mohena bhārata
sarva-bhūtāni sammohaṁ sarge yānti parantapa#O descendant of Bharat
column2: the dualities of desire and aversion arise from illusion. O conqueror of enemies
column3: all living beings in the material realm are deluded by these.#ichchhā—desire; dveṣha—aversion; samutthena—arise from; dvandva—of duality; mohena—from the illusion; bhārata—Arjuna
column4: descendant of Bharat; sarva—all; bhūtāni—living beings; sammoham—into delusion; sarge—since birth; yānti—enter; parantapa—Arjuna

column1:
BG 7.28#येषाम् तु अन्तगतं पापम् 
जनानाम् पुण्य कर्मणाम् |
ते द्वन्द्व मोह निर्मुक्ताः 
भजन्ते माम् दृढ व्रताः ||28||#yeṣhāṁ tvanta-gataṁ pāpaṁ janānāṁ puṇya-karmaṇām
te dvandva-moha-nirmuktā bhajante māṁ dṛiḍha-vratāḥ#But persons
column2: whose sins have been destroyed by engaging in pious activities
column3: become free from the illusion of dualities. Such persons worship Me with determination.#yeṣhām—whose; tu—but; anta-gatam—completely destroyed; pāpam—sins; janānām—of persons; puṇya—pious; karmaṇām—activities; te—they; dvandva—of dualities; moha—illusion; nirmuktāḥ—free from; bhajante—worship; mām—Me; dṛiḍha-vratāḥ—with determination#

column1:
BG 7.29#जरा मरण मोक्षाय 
माम् आश्रित्य यतन्ति ये |
ते ब्रह्म तत् विदुः कृत्स्नम् 
अध्यात्मम् कर्म च अखिलम् ||29||#jarā-maraṇa-mokṣhāya mām āśhritya yatanti ye
te brahma tadviduḥ kṛitsnam adhyātmaṁ karma chākhilam#Those who take shelter in Me
column2: striving for liberation from old-age and death
column3: come to know the Brahman
column5: and the entire field of karmic action.#jarā—from old age; maraṇa—and death; mokṣhāya—for liberation; mām—me; āśhritya—take shelter in; yatanti—strive; ye—who; te—they; brahma—Brahman; tat—that; viduḥ—know; kṛitsnam—everything; adhyātmam—the individual self; karma—karmic action; cha—and; akhilam—entire#

column1:
BG 7.30#स अधिभूत अधिदैवम् माम् 
स अधियज्ञम् च ये विदुः |
प्रयाण काले अपि च माम् 
ते विदुः युक्त चेतसः ||30||#sādhibhūtādhidaivaṁ māṁ sādhiyajñaṁ cha ye viduḥ
prayāṇa-kāle ’pi cha māṁ te vidur yukta-chetasaḥ#Those who know Me as the governing principle of the adhibhūta (field of matter) and the adhidaiva (the celestial gods)
column2: and as adhiyajña (the Lord of all sacrificial performances)
column3: such enlightened souls are in full consciousness of Me even at the time of death.#sa-adhibhūta—governing principle of the field of matter; adhidaivam—governing principle of the celestial gods; mām—me; sa-adhiyajñam—governing principle of the Lord of all sacrificial performances; cha—and; ye—who; viduḥ—know; prayāṇa—of death; kāle—at the time; api—even; cha—and; mām—me; te—they; viduḥ—know; yukta-chetasaḥ—in full consciousness of me#

column1:
BG 7.E#ॐ तत् सत् इति 
श्रीमत् भगवत् गीतासु उपनिषत्सु
ब्रह्म विद्यायाम् योग शास्त्रे श्रीकृष्ण अर्जुन संवादे
ज्ञान विज्ञान योगः नाम सप्तमः अध्यायः||#Om tat sat iti
Srimad Bhagavad Gitasu Upanishatsu
Brahma Vidyayam yogashastre Sri-Krishna-arujuna samvade
Jnana Vijnana Yogo Nama saptamodhyaha#Om Truth Is. Thus in the Upanishads of the glorious Bhagavad Gita
column2: the science of the Eternal
column3: the scripture of Yoga
column4: the dialogue between Sri Krishna and Arjuna ends the Seventh discourse entitled The Yoga of Knowledge and Realisation.#Om—Om; tat—That; sat—truth; iti—is; Srimad—Auspicious one; Bhagavad—said by lord; Gitasu—song; Upanishatsu—upanishad; Brahma Vidyayam—contains knowledge of Brahman; yogashastre—contains technology of removing duality; Sri Krishna arujuna samvade—a dialogue between Krishna and Arjuna; Jnana Vijnana Yogo Nama—Yoga of Knowledge and Realisation; saptamodhyaha—Seventh chapter#
